ead.archdesc:
======= RMM01972Edwin Thomas Gibson papers1972Compiled by H.T. HickersonDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
April 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
cmrce agricThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:14:36 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Edwin Thomas Gibson papers
Gibson, Edwin Thomas, 1884-1959.
1972
2.1 cubic feet. 2.1 cubic feet.
1935-1958. Correspondence, manuscripts, notebooks, pamphlets. Correspondence, manuscript or typescript speeches and notebooks, and pamphlets and other printed matter concerning national security, amomg other things. Collection material in English 2081753
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
H.T. Hickerson
Date completed:
June 1982
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, April 2004
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Lawyer, Cornell University trustee.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, manuscript or typescript speeches and notebooks, and pamphlets and other printed matter concerning the Mutual Security Act and Mutual Security Administration, Defense Production Administration, Market Research Corporation of America, National Planning Association, the American Assembly, International Materials Conference, International Milk Processors, National Biscuit Company, Merchants Refridgerating Company, First Savings (Federal) and Loan Association, and the Eisenhower Exchange Fellowship; also, letters and other papers concerning his position as a trustee of Cornell University (1952-57) and his interest in the Cornell Alumni Association in New York City. Correspondents include Robert Bruce Chiperfield, Dwight D. Eisenhower, Eli Ginzberg, Harold E. Stassen, Charles E. Wilson, and Henry M. Wriston.
Cite As:
Edwin Thomas Gibson papers, #1972. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Speeches. United States -- Politics and government. Lawyers. Marketing research. Dairying. Commerce. Banks and banking. Chiperfield, Robert Bruce. Eisenhower, Dwight D. (Dwight David), 1890-1969. Ginzberg, Eli, 1911-2002. Stassen, Harold E. (Harold Edward), 1907-2001. Wilson, Charles E. Wriston, Henry M. American Assembly Cornell University -- : Trustees. Cornell University. Alumni Association Eisenhower Exchange Fellowship First Savings Federal and Loan Association International Milk Processors Market Research Corporation of America National Biscuit Company National Planning Association United States. Defense Production Administration United States. Mutual Security Agency International Materials Conference
2081753 <<<<<<< HEAD "National Biscuit Co., Etc."1
Scope and Contents
"Legal Paper - Historic in their Value." Contains printed material and typed briefs about the National Biscuit Co."Industry Committee for Consumer" (Black Notebook)1
Scope and Contents
"Saving - Expenditure Research." Typed manuscript titled "Plan for Development of Optimum Survey Techniques for the Current Measurement of Consumer Savings, Income and Expenditures." Notebook also includes a pamphlet titled "The Effect of Respondent Ingorance on Survey Results""Mariot Research Corporation of America"1
Scope and Contents
Contains printed report of MRCA functions. Divided as follows: introduction, engineering analysis of needs, market situation analysis, controlled tests and experiments, strategy, theory and systems, training and education, organization chartsUntitledMay 6, 19541
Scope and Contents
Typed manuscript titled "MRCA Retirement Pension and Profit Sharing Plans." Presented to the board of directors on May 6, 1954"Market Research,"1954-19571
Scope and Contents
Industry Committee - Contains correspondence between Gibson and various other people"Market Research,"1956-19581
Scope and Contents
Industry Committee - Correspondence"Europe - Personal,"Mar. 24, 19531
Scope and Contents
Contains the master copy, with revisions, of an "Evaluation Report" sent to Hon. Harold E. Stassen, Director of Mutual Security, Washington D.C. Also several other printed copies of the same report. Much of Gibson's personal material relating to the trip to Europe, i.e. letter about passport, hotel bills while in Europe, schedule of stopping points in Europe and times of meetings and with whom, etc. Trip began on Feb. 20, 1953 and ended on Mar. 15, 1953. The purpose of the trip was to get information for the Mutual Security Act."M.S.A." (Mutual Security Act),19531
Scope and Contents
Contains printed material of a draft of a bill for the Mutual Security Act and Congressional Committee reports on this bill. Also there is some correspondence, i.e. to and from Congressman Chiperfield, chairman of the foreign affairs committee"1st Fed. - Executive Study Committee,"1958July 16, 19581
Scope and Contents
Contains printed material and correspondence. Typed report of "Counsel's Memorandum re Report of Ad Hoc Committee" dated July 16, 1958 . Correspondence concerns the Ad Hoc Committee of which Gibson was chairman. Also includes photostats of a letter, a report titled "Consultive Service Contract with President Mortlock," a typed report titled "First Federal Saving and Loan Association of New York Employees' Deferred Profit Sharing Plan and Trust," and a printed report titled "The Chase Manhattan Employees' Benefit Program""Mutual Security Administration"Mar. 24, 195319531
Scope and Contents
Contains printed letter dated Mar. 24, 1953 , to Harold E. Stassen from a group of buisness executives including Gibson as deputy chairman. The purpose of the report was to evaluate the Mutual Security program and personnel in various countries.
Also written manuscript material by Gibson and other printed reports. Diary of Gibson's trip to Europe in 1953 , having to do with the M.S.A. Commentary on his meetings with the various team captains. Rough draft of letter from Gibson to Congressman Chiperfield, chairman of the Committee on foreign affairs. Also other printed and manuscript material relating to M.S.A."Speeches - E.T. Gibson,"1935-19571"Speeches - Washington - D.P.A."19511
Scope and Contents
All the speeches concern the Defense Production Administration, 1951What Makes an Executive?195?1
Scope and Contents
Written by a group of men headed by Eli Ginsburg. He was chairman of a round table on executive potential and performance. New York, Columbia Press, 195?"E.T. Gibson - Miscellaneous,"1940-19551
Scope and Contents
Most material in the 1950's"National Planning Association,"1954-19562
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, mimeo reports and correspondence"First Federal Savings and Loan Assn. - Retirement Plan,"19562
Scope and Contents
Some correspondence. Also photostatic copy of "Summary of a Revised Progam for the First Federal Savings and Loan Association of New York." Printed draft of report titled "First Federal Savings and Loan Association of New York Employees' Deferred Profit Sharing Plan.""Pennsylvania State University,"1956-1957July 9, 1956June 19592
Scope and Contents
Correspondence. Concerns Gibson's talk titled "Effects of World Conditions on Buisness," at the Executive Management program on July 9, 1956 , and in June 1959"Market Research Corporation of America,"1958Sept. 30, 19582
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, memos, reports. "Report to Financial Review Board from National Consumer Panel as of Sept. 30, 1958"Merchants Refrigerating Company,"1952-19542
Scope and Contents
Correspondence concerning the company. Gibson was a director."Refrigeration Research Foundation,"1952-19542
Scope and Contents
Correspondence concerning Merchants Refrigerating Company. Also includes some pamphlets.Dr. Edwin T. Gibson, "Merchants - Expansion Data,"1956-19582
Scope and Contents
Correspondence concerning the Merchants Refrigerating Company"Edwin T. Gibson - Biography"2"Market Research Corporation of America"Aug. 19582
Scope and Contents
Report titled "Past Performance, Current Budget, and Future Projection," Aug. 1958 . Also "Schedule of Financial Tables""Dr. Newman - Round Table"2
Scope and Contents
Includes printed reports of the eight meetings held by William H. Newman on "Management of Expanding Organizations""The American Assembly" (Black Notebook)2
Scope and Contents
Printed material concerning the Assembly, its trustees, charter by-laws, Columbia - Assembly agreement, sketches of Assemblies, minutes of meetings, and statements of accounts"Columbia - Exec. Program,"June 21, 1957June 19572
Scope and Contents
Notes and correspondence concerning the Executive Program in Buisness, June 1957"Cornell,"1951-19532
Scope and Contents
Correspondence concerning the Cornell Alumni Assoc. in New York City; Elwyn E. Seelye, Maxwell Upson, Hiag K. Shiroyan, Phillips Wyman, etc."Special Com. Cornell"2
Scope and Contents
Correspondence and reports concerning the special committee and report mande by the Trustees"Cornell University" (carbon)1952-1957Feb. 28, 19562
Scope and Contents
Typescript report titled "Report of Alumni Trustee Edwin T. Gibson, 1952-1957 ." Also copy of Arthur H. Dean's Remarks at Cornell Legislative Dinner, Albany, New York, Feb. 28, 1956 , 22 pp. Xerox"Market Research Corp. of America,"1957-19592
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, memos, and reports concerning the M.R.C. of America"Washington Service File - E.T. Gibson"1951-19522
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, 1951-1952 . Concerns the Defense Production Administration"Defense Production Administration Organization,"1951-19522
Scope and Contents
Correspondence including letter from Charles E. Wilson, director of defense mobilization. Legal forms and other letters having to do with the D.P.A."International Material Conference,"1951-19532
Scope and Contents
Correspondence concerning the International Materials Conference. Mostly letters about Gibson's leaving the I.M.C."First Federal Savings and Loan Assn. - Columbia Report"Nov. 28, 19562
Scope and Contents
Printed material with the following titles - "Characteristics and Attitude of Depositors;" "The Role of Dividends in Savings Behavior;" "First Federal Savings and Loan Assoc. of N.Y. Revised to Nov. 28, 1956 "Report2
Scope and Contents
Report titled "Specifications for and Location of Frozen Foods Service Complex for Metropolitan New York." Report to Merchants Refrigerating Co."E.T. Gibson - 1st. Fed."2
Scope and Contents
Letters and reports concerning the First Federal Savings and Loan Assn. in New York, and also the Ad Hoc Committee Report"American Assembly,"Feb. 1953-1958Apr. 13, 1954Oct. 22, 19542
Scope and Contents
Correspondence concerning the American Assembly including letters from Henry M. Wreston, President of the American Assembly, and President Eisenhower. The Eisenhower letters are dated Apr. 13, 1954 and Oct. 22, 1954Annual Publication - Frozen Food Book and Directory2
Scope and Contents
Published by the National Frozen Food Distributors Association1957-1958 Manual - "Cornell Univerity Council"1957-19582"Directory of Membership"Feb. 19582
Scope and Contents
The National Council of the National Planning Association as of Feb. 1958"First Federal Savings and Loan Assn. Special Law Com."1957-19582
Scope and Contents
General Correspondence, 1957-1958"Merchants - Finance Com."2
Scope and Contents
Printed material concerning the Market Research Corporation of America""Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Inc."Sept. 15, 19582
Scope and Contents
Correspondence. Minutes of the annual meeting of the Board of Trustees, Sept. 15, 1958"Eisenhower Fellowship,"1957-19582
Scope and Contents
Letters, progress reports on 1959 program, statements of assets etc., for 1957-1958, list of Eisenhower Exchange Fellows for 1958, and other buisness correspondenceDr. Ginsberg's Round Table"2
Scope and Contents
Minutes of the eight meetings. Also includes other correspondence relating to the round table conferences"International Milk Processors Inc."2
Scope and Contents
Correspondence and notes about the International Milk Processors Inc."The Refrigeration Research Foundation,"1953-19562
Scope and Contents
Correspondence, reports, and bulletins. Also printed copy of the report of the directors for 1952 of the Refrigeration Research Foundation"Ginsberg - Human Resources,"1957-19582
Scope and Contents
Correspondence mainly from Eli Ginsberg"Quick Frozen Foods" (Pamphlet),1929-19542Letter to Gibson from Thomas B. McCabe, chairman of the Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Inc., Jan. 17, 1955 ; 6 pp. including attachmentsJan. 17, 19552Report of Operations of the Eisenhower Exchange Fellowships, Inc., for the year 1954 . 7 pp. mimeographed, including attachments19542Miscellaneous2
Scope and Contents
"Edwin T. Gibson Papers" (declassified by the U.S. State Dept.); Printed typescript, "General Missions, Functions and Activities of MAAGs" (Military Assistance Advisory Groups); Printed pamphlet, "Planning Production for Defense;" Printed list, "Chiefs of MAAGs and Service Sections of MAAGs; Printed typescript, "Belgium;" Printed typescript, "Military Procurement under the Mutual Security Program;" Telegram (date, sender, recipient, information, cut out); Dittoed report, "My Conclusions," 2 pp.Edwin Thomas GibsonApr. 14, 19561951-19573
Scope and Contents
This box contains records of Special Trustee Committee of Inquiry established Apr. 14, 1956 , to investigate possible Communist sympathies and activities of Professor Philip Morrison, Laboratory of Nuclear Studies, Cornell. The records cover 1951-1957 .
Conditions Governing Access
Restricted to permission of archivist for 30 years from date of creation (expires Jan. 31, 1987) ======= "National Biscuit Co., Etc."1

Much of Gibson's personal material relating to the trip to Europe, i.e. letter about passport, hotel bills while in Europe, schedule of stopping points in Europe and times of meetings and with whom, etc. Trip began on Feb. 20, 1953 and ended on Mar. 15, 1953. The purpose of the trip was to get information for the Mutual Security Act."M.S.A." (Mutual Security Act),19531

Contains printed letter dated Mar. 24, 1953 , to Harold E. Stassen from a group of buisness executives including Gibson as deputy chairman. The purpose of the report was to evaluate the Mutual Security program and personnel in various countries.
Also written manuscript material by Gibson and other printed reports. Diary of Gibson's trip to Europe in 1953 , having to do with the M.S.A. Commentary on his meetings with the various team captains. Rough draft of letter from Gibson to Congressman Chiperfield, chairman of the Committee on foreign affairs. "Edwin T. Gibson Papers" (declassified by the U.S. State Dept.); Printed typescript, "General Missions, Functions and Activities of MAAGs" (Military Assistance Advisory Groups); Printed pamphlet, "Planning Production for Defense;" Printed list, "Chiefs of MAAGs and Service Sections of MAAGs; Printed typescript, "Belgium;" Printed typescript, "Military Procurement under the Mutual Security Program;" Telegram (date, sender, recipient, information, cut out); Dittoed report, "My Conclusions," 2 pp.Edwin Thomas GibsonApr. 14, 19561951-19573
Scope and Contents
This box contains records of Special Trustee Committee of Inquiry established Apr. 14, 1956 , to investigate possible Communist sympathies and activities of Professor Philip Morrison, Laboratory of Nuclear Studies, Cornell. The records cover 1951-1957 .
